mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance or negative mean returns during periods of falling FEDFUNDS over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points within a single month signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to produce a negative rank IC over any 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand growth that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a rank IC above 0.04, or the interaction term loses significance in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Contraction in the index of industrial production during periods of high inflation reflects supply-side bottlenecks that compress corporate margins and generate negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during high inflation periods produce positive or flat 20-day forward returns, or the predictive power vanishes when CPIAUCSL growth falls below 2.5% annually.

mechanism_narrative: A cut in the federal funds rate that exceeds the monthly inflation rate signals a real rate compression that immediately boosts equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS decline exceeds CPIAUCSL monthly change
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of instances where the real rate compression trigger activates, or the signal shows zero predictive accuracy over a 2-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment indicates structural labor market weakness that suppresses corporate earnings expectations and produces negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence fails to predict negative 60-day returns with statistical significance, or positive returns dominate during periods of confirmed labor market deterioration.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects expanding aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no correlation with 20-day forward returns, or the rank IC remains below 0.02 across all rolling 3-year windows in the sample period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in industrial production alongside falling retail sales during a liquidity crunch signals immediate demand destruction that forces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive during synchronized supply and demand contractions, or the mechanism fails to produce a negative directional accuracy above 50% in stress regimes.
